refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting achievement of a productivity target. It is expressed in terms of what the system must do (support achieving productivity targets) without specifying how. The units (m³/h or ton/h) are part of the measurement context, not implementation details. Therefore, the FR is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection that the NGHE solution enables achieving or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target over the agreed evaluation period under representative conditions.

candidate_dps: Adjust processing rate parameter to meet productivity target
Implement feedback control loop to monitor and adjust throughput dynamically
Allocate resources dynamically based on real-time productivity measurements
Schedule operations to optimize volume or mass throughput within safety and policy constraints
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement feedback control loop to monitor and adjust throughput dynamically

coupling_check.notes: The feedback control loop DP is logically independent and can adjust throughput without tightly coupling to other system parameters, supporting modularity and adaptability.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely measurement of productivity is available
Operational policies and safety plans allow dynamic adjustment of processing rates
Feedback control can respond quickly enough to maintain productivity targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including measurement and control of productivity metrics in the FR to clarify the need for feedback mechanisms.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic and control algorithms responsible for managing system throughput and performance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data for monitoring throughput and sends status updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and sends control adjustments to actuators or subsystems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as feedback control logic is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as control loop primarily interacts internally and with telemetry/tasking interfaces.
Vehicle module may implement some control but feedback loop logic is broader than vehicle-specific functions, causing diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and user commands, not internal feedback control loops, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not throughput control, resulting in weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The feedback control loop for dynamic throughput adjustment is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes system control logic and interfaces directly with telemetry and tasking for monitoring and actuation.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some control logic is embedded there, causing partial diffusion.
Dependency on Telemetry and Tasking interfaces requires robust interface management to avoid coupling issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement feedback control loop to monitor and adjust throughput dynamically' directly addresses the FR of supporting achievement of customer-defined productivity targets by dynamically adjusting throughput, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of maintaining productivity ≥ target during operations.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some control logic is embedded there, causing partial diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2) for the feedback control loop, indicating good architectural packaging. However, some diffusion risk exists due to partial control logic in the Vehicle module and dependencies on Telemetry and Tasking interfaces, which require careful interface management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring reported productivity against the target during representative operations, leveraging telemetry data and control feedback within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The feedback control loop is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the core system logic and control algorithms, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry and tasking.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the boundary and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to minimize diffusion risk.
Strengthen interface management protocols for Telemetry and Tasking to reduce coupling and ensure robust control loop operation.
Consider consolidating any scattered control logic currently in Vehicle or other modules into NGHE Solution to improve containment and reduce diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module with good containment and feasible verification. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to partial control logic in the Vehicle module and dependencies on Telemetry and Tasking interfaces, further decomposition of the FR into sub-FRs is warranted. This will allow more precise allocation and control of responsibilities, reduce diffusion risk, and improve safety assurance by enabling targeted verification and interface management at a finer granularity.
next_level_sub_frs: Adjust throughput dynamically based on feedback

revise.why: Clarify and document the boundary and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to minimize diffusion risk, and strengthen interface management protocols for Telemetry and Tasking to reduce coupling and ensure robust control loop operation.

assumptions: The current FR and DP adequately capture the high-level control loop functionality.
The NGHE Solution module is the primary owner of the control logic.
Partial diffusion risk arises mainly from control logic embedded in the Vehicle module and interface dependencies.
open_risks: Potential safety impact due to diffusion of control logic across modules.
Interface coupling may affect robustness of control loop operation.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Verification data showing control loop performance and containment effectiveness.
Documentation of control logic allocation across modules.
